_schema.description: Per-project baseline for `dazzle validate` (lint_appspec) output. Keys are project directories relative to the repo root. Values pin the expected error and warning counts so deliberate fixture violations (kitchen-sink test data) stay out of the regression-noise channel.
drift_rules: actual error count < baseline → FAIL (issue fixed; remove from baseline).
actual warning count > baseline + grace (10) → FAIL (new warning surface).
expected_error_patterns: each substring must appear ≥1 time in the actual error list.

examples/invoice_ops.rationale: invoice_ops is the keystone migration-story example. The 11 warnings are: 1 decimal monetary field (LineItem.unit_amount), 2 approval-gate preview notices, 2 audit-enabled info notices (audit+field-changes), and 7 fitness.repr_fields advisory notices (1 per audited/scoped entity). These are known, expected, and tracking the DSL design state.

expected_error_patterns: uses 'decimal' type for monetary value. Use 'money' type instead
fixtures/pra.rationale: pra is the largest example, kept as a kitchen-sink exercise of every DSL form including decimal stream-schema fields. The 7 errors are the validator's money-only rule (#1061) firing on the 7 deliberate decimal `amount:` declarations in orders_intent / payments_intent / orders_fact / payments_fact / ledger_fact streams. The 350+ warnings are entities with permit-but-no-scope and adjacent default-deny gates by design plus 15 #1324-FR-6 nav auto-discovery-reliance notices (personas with no `uses nav`) — fixing these would lose the example's coverage. PLUS 7 route-collision errors (#1489): the corpus deliberately declares multiple surfaces sharing the same (mode, entity) — e.g. multiple `view` surfaces on Invoice, list+kanban on Task — which all resolve to the same auto-route and collide. Real apps must use one surface per (mode, entity) (secondary views go in workspace regions); the corpus keeps them for parser coverage. (Was 12/19 before #1512: 5 of those surfaces are consumed only as experience/process step targets, which render inline by name and never claim the auto-route, so they are no longer flagged.)

examples/acme_billing.rationale: Includes 5 #1324-FR-6 nav auto-discovery-reliance notices (personas with no `uses nav`) — these are informational nudges, not defects.

examples/hr_records.rationale: Includes 4 #1324-FR-6 nav auto-discovery-reliance notices (personas with no `uses nav`) — these are informational nudges, not defects.

fixtures/shapes_validation.rationale: #1355 fixed Shape's dead non-oracle scope rules (permits added, blanket forbid removed, material != shadow conjuncts express the documented intent) — the 8 [RBAC scope_without_permit] warnings are gone. Current 20 = fitness/nav advisories + oracle no_scope_rule create/update/delete probes + 1 [RBAC orphan_role] on 'outsider', which is deliberate: outsider is the deny-all baseline persona and now relies purely on default-deny (re-adding a forbid for it would just trade orphan_role for redundant_forbid).
